t_ch: 可惜…我着实力不从心，推开窗子时竟发出了吱呀一响。
t_en: Unfortunately... Things didn't go quite as planned... When I opened the window, it let out a squeaking noise.

t_ch: 顿时七八个祭司把我团团围住，对我又是咒骂又是洒蜜酿，还用大地兽鬃毛做成的笤帚往我脸上直扑。我好不容易才抽身。
t_en: In an instant, seven or eight priests surrounded me, cursing me and covering me in ambrosia. They even struck me with brushes made from dromas fur! I was lucky to make it out of there...

t_ch: 后来才知道，原来「神偷」的传闻，也不过是引我出马的伎俩。这帮老骨头什么时候竟这么聪明了？
t_en: It was only later that I found out that these rumors of a "master thief" were just a ploy to lure me into making a move... When did these old crones get so cunning?

t_ch: 或许你只是不在状态。
t_en: Maybe you're just off your game?

t_ch: 这就是上天的报应啊！
t_en: That's divine retribution!

t_ch: 唉…希望您永远没有这样的体验。一向仰赖的才能在刹那间被抽走，滴水不漏的手法顿时漏洞百出，自己好像不是自己了似的。
t_en: Ah... I hope you never have to experience something like that. The talents that I have relied on over the years vanished in an instant. My once perfectly watertight handiwork was suddenly full of holes... It's like I wasn't even myself.

t_ch: 现在，我连个宝箱也不能从您手上偷走。这样的我，干脆跳下重渊，游到塞纳托斯面前，让它给我个痛快好了……
t_en: Now, I can't even pry a single treasure from your hands... As I am now, I may as well jump into the Abyss, swim up to Thanatos, and let them put me out of my misery...

t_ch: 宝箱送你还不成吗！
t_en: Just take the chest!

t_ch: 要勇敢地面对困难。
t_en: You can't let challenges get you down!

t_ch: 你以前也没偷成过…
t_en: But you didn't manage to steal it before, either...

t_ch: 真没想到，我陷入迷茫时，您竟愿意出言安慰。我着实无以为报，就用一个秘密来答谢您的善意吧——
t_en: I never thought that when I was at my most lost, you would offer me words of comfort. I have nothing to repay you with, so allow me to reward your kindness with a secret...

t_ch: 元老院大殿，压根没有拱心石！
t_en: The Council of Elders' main hall doesn't even have a keystone!

t_ch: 桀桀桀桀，看您的反应，可比偷宝箱有趣一万倍！宝箱就当您和我演这出戏的谢礼吧，回头见！
t_en: Hehehehe! That reaction of yours is a million times more thrilling than stealing some chest! Take it as a thank you for taking part in this little performance with me. See ya!

t_ch: 唔…头好晕……您对自己试过这招吗？
t_en: Ugh... I'm so dizzy... Have you tried that move on yourself before?

t_ch: 有什么必要吗？
t_en: Why would I do that?

t_ch: 每次假期结束前都想试试…
t_en: I always toy with the idea when my holiday is about to end...

t_ch: 您假使试过，一定会觉得这招太不人道了！
t_en: If you try it on yourself, you'll realize just how inhumane it is!

t_ch: 很难描述那种感觉…就好像自己是副被清洗的手套，衬里朝外翻了个面，感觉和思绪都颠倒了。
t_en: It's difficult to describe this feeling... I feel like a pair of gloves that has been washed and turned inside-out... My mind feels like it's been completely flipped around.

t_ch: 越是向前狂奔，就越是往后倒退。想骂上一声，污言秽语反倒钻进了自己的肚子！
t_en: The more I try to rush forwards, the more I end up going backwards. I want to swear at you but all my dirty words feel like they're rushing into my stomach instead!

t_ch: 还好我是洗手套的人。
t_en: At least I wash my gloves.

t_ch: 那你现在应该吃饱了？
t_en: Sounds like you're getting a good meal finally?

t_ch: 你…你！你这个冷酷无情的人！这种滥用权能的行径，着实让人不齿！
t_en: You... You! You are heartless and cruel! Such abuse of power is truly revolting!

t_ch: 就在这「万径之门」前，我得给点颜色瞧瞧，教欧洛尼斯知道，玩弄这种把戏是什么下场！
t_en: Right here, before the Gate of Infinity, I'll teach you a proper lesson! I'll show Oronyx what happens if they play these games!

t_ch: 呃啊，不公平，一定是欧洛尼斯在暗中相助！
t_en: Aghh, that's not fair... Oronyx must have been helping you from the shadows!

t_ch: 别高兴得太早，等换个地方，有你好看的！
t_en: Don't be too pleased with yourself! Fight me somewhere else and you'll see!

t_ch: 呜啊…这是怎么办到的？
t_en: Ooh, err... How did you do that?

t_ch: 明明已经钻进了「盗洞」，一眨眼居然又回到了原处…吓、吓死我了！难道说，您是欧洛尼斯的眷从吗？
t_en: I saw you burrowing into the Thieves' Tunnel, but you returned to the same spot in the blink of an eye... I—It gave me a fright! Could you be Oronyx's kin?

t_ch: 我是欧洛尼斯的眷从。
t_en: I am Oronyx's kin.

t_ch: 我就说呢！这一手本领可不是凡人能有的。
t_en: I knew it! No mortal could have such powers.

t_ch: 我是欧洛尼斯本尊。
t_en: I am Oronyx in the flesh.

t_ch: 您竟纡尊降贵，凭附到了凡人身上，难怪我认不出来呢。
t_en: To have descended from godhood and possessed a mortal shell, it's no wonder I didn't recognize you.

t_ch: 我是、银河球棒侠！
t_en: I am, the Galactic Baseballer!

t_ch: 银、银河球棒侠大人，久仰！
t_en: Th—The Galactic Baseballer! I've heard so much about you!

t_ch: 抱歉不知晓您的身份，多有得罪。我找到这位墨涅塔的织者，也只是想求助于它的智慧。您找它一定更有要事，就先交还于您吧！
t_en: I apologize for not recognizing you, please forgive me. I sought this weaver of Mnestia simply to seek its wisdom. If you have more urgent business, please go ahead!

t_ch: 这就相信了？
t_en: You believed me that easily?

t_ch: 这回的策略是装傻？
t_en: Is the plan this time to play dumb?

t_ch: 原来您只是在讲玩笑话吗！真是吓了我一跳。
t_en: You were just kidding? You gave me such a fright.

t_ch: 既然您不愿意透露身份，那我也不再追问了。不过，您好像认得我？
t_en: If you're unwilling to reveal your identity, I would not pursue the matter. But you seem to recognize me?

t_ch: 声名鼎鼎的江洋大盗。
t_en: You're the infamous bandit hero.

t_ch: 您太抬举我了！我不过是个初出茅庐的小贼，和真正的行家里手还差得远呢。
t_en: You flatter me! I'm but an amateur thief, a far cry from the true masters of the craft.

t_ch: 一次也没能得手的小贼。
t_en: A petty thief that's never succeeded.

t_ch: 的确…我不过是个初出茅庐的小贼，和真正的行家里手还差得远呢。
t_en: It's true... I'm but an amateur thief, a far cry from the true masters of the craft.

t_ch: 看来您也不知道更多……
t_en: Seems like you don't know much else either...

t_ch: 唉，这里的氛围实在让人毛骨悚然。一帮鬼迷心窍的战士，像机器一样厮杀赴死。就连墙上的雕塑都会说话，说些什么「勇者…勇者…再靠近些」之类的……
t_en: *sigh* The air around here sure is eerie. A band of warriors mechanically fighting to their deaths as if possessed by madness. Even the sculptures on the walls can talk, whispering things like "Warrior... Warrior... Come closer"...

t_ch: 一看就是陷阱吧！谁敢靠近啊？
t_en: It's obviously a trap! Who'd dare go close?

t_ch: 我好像还听到了大地深处的嗫嚅，声音沙哑，语句零碎…其中的蕴意，我甚至不敢转述。您最好也尽快离开吧！
t_en: I thought I also heard halting whispers from the depths of the earth, its voice husky, words faltering... I don't even dare mention what they may mean. You'd better get out of here fast!

t_ch: 呜啊，好险！差点就掉进熔岩，被塞纳托斯的手捞走了！
t_en: Aahhh, that was close! I almost fell into the lava, snatched by Thanatos' hand!

t_ch: 话说回来，「熔岩」究竟受塞纳托斯掌控，还是归法吉娜女士管辖呢……
t_en: Speaking of which, is lava governed by Thanatos, or Lady Phagousa...

t_ch: 悬锋城人把城市建立在这儿，又出于什么考量？就算他们真的这么爱脚底板发烫的感觉，至少也给悬桥加个扶手吧？
t_en: What were the people of Castrum Kremnos thinking when they built their city here? Even if they really enjoyed having their feet scorched, you'd think they would at least add railings to their bridges.

t_ch: 怎么这么多问题？
t_en: Why so many questions?

t_ch: 你是什么好奇宝宝吗？
t_en: You a curious creature or what?

t_ch: 好奇总没有错吧？您在看到宝箱时，难道能按耐住这种迫切的心情，想要打开它一探究竟？
t_en: It never hurts to be curious. When you see a treasure chest, can you suppress that rising impulse to open it and see what's inside?

t_ch: 您看起来不像悬锋城人，莫非也是来参加祭典的战士？
t_en: You don't look like someone from Castrum Kremnos. Could you be a warrior here to participate in the festival?

t_ch: 我来自未来。
t_en: I come from the future.

t_ch: 我来自过去。
t_en: I came from the past.

t_ch: 我来自世界之外。
t_en: I come from beyond worlds.

t_ch: 您要是不想回答，大可以保持沉默，没必要随口乱说！
t_en: You could've just remained silent if you didn't feel like answering, instead of spouting rubbish!

t_ch: 就、就算我初来乍到，也不是谁都可以随便欺负的！
t_en: E—Even if I'm new around here, I'm not to be trifled with!

t_ch: 嘁…满嘴跑马车的家伙，竟然还有两下子！
t_en: Whew... You sure got some skills for someone who talks tough!

t_ch: 我奈何不了你…但是塔兰顿一定会惩罚你！
t_en: I may not be a match for you... but Talanton will surely punish you!

t_ch: 哟，是您啊。
t_en: Ooh, it's you.

t_ch: 桀桀——既没挑拨指针拉扯咱的后背，也没有揪着咱的尾巴不放，第一次这么平淡地打招呼，竟然还有些不适应。
t_en: Crrrk... For us to be calmly greeting each other for the first time without poking at our backs with a compass, nor pulling at each other's tails, I'm suddenly not used to this.

t_ch: 又在打什么坏主意？
t_en: What're you up to this time?

t_ch: 各行各业都需要充足的休息，这是塔兰顿赋予世间一切生灵的权利。
t_en: All lines of work need ample rest. This is the right Talanton granted all creatures of the world.

t_ch: 所以，如您所见——我正在休息呢。
t_en: So, as you can see... I'm taking a break.

t_ch: 我什么时候揪过你的尾巴？
t_en: When have I ever pulled on your tail?

t_ch: 扎格列斯老祖有云：「一个人就是整个世界，一个人揪过就是所有人都揪过。」
t_en: Our ancestor Zagreus once said: "One person is an entire world, one person's pulling means everyone's pulling."

t_ch: 说起来，我这么清闲，您实在功不可没。您总是在出人意料的时刻打断我的火热手感，害得我都提不起劲了。
t_en: Come to think of it, you're to blame for my idleness right now. You keep appearing at the most inopportune moments and interrupting my hot streak — I can't even get into the groove of things now.

t_ch: 这段时间，咱还是先金盆洗手，找份正经工作吧——您有什么推荐吗？
t_en: During this period, we should turn over a new leaf and find a proper job. Got any recommendations?

t_ch: 当防盗大使。
t_en: Become an anti-theft ambassador.

t_ch: 教他们把财宝全都存进地窖，也省得我到时候复出时到处翻找。是个好主意。
t_en: Teach them to store their valuables in their cellars, so I won't have to rummage through the whole house while I'm at it. Good idea.

t_ch: 当浴池吉祥物。
t_en: Become a bath mascot.

t_ch: 法吉娜女士当年和我诚然关系不错。她的宴席上，我的祝词总能让大伙笑得前仰后合。
t_en: Phagousa and I enjoyed great relations back in the day. At their banquet, my compliments had everyone roaring with laughter.

t_ch: 桀桀——每回他们喝得不省人事，我就偷偷摘走一颗浪尖上的珍珠。她直到最后还没发现，还疑心浪头怎么秃顶了。
t_en: Crrrkk... They'd be blackout drunk every time, then I'd steal away a pearl from the crest of the wave. She never noticed, and even thought the wave was balding.

t_ch: 当搬运工吧，老巴特。
t_en: Be a transport worker, Old Bart.

t_ch: 您还记得这回事？都是把东西从一个地方搬到另一个地方，我没准真挺拿手的也说不定。
t_en: You still remember that? It's just moving things from one place to another. I bet I'd be great at it.

t_ch: 晴空、青草、遛弯的公民们…扎格列斯老祖有云：「无所事事的午后，连思考都是罪过。」
t_en: Clear skies, green grass, citizens out and about... Our ancestor Zagreus once said: "The slightest thought is a sin on an idle afternoon."

t_ch: 正事暂且放到一边，您要是有什么想聊的，咱陪你唠唠也无妨。
t_en: Let's leave that matter aside for now and tell me what you wanted to talk about. I'll banter with you.

t_ch: 关于你的事迹…
t_en: Tell me your story...

t_ch: 哈哈，我的盗窃生涯，比墨涅塔的金丝连起来还长；我偷过的东西，比艾格勒的眼睛更多。
t_en: Haha, my tales of thievery are even longer than Mnestia's golden thread... I've stolen more things than Aquila has eyes.

t_ch: 您想听哪一类，是最有惊无险的、还是我最引以为豪的？
t_en: What do you wish to hear? The most harrowing heist, or my proudest feat?

t_ch: 最有惊无险的吧。
t_en: The most harrowing heist.

t_ch: 最惊险的，莫过于那次——塞纳托斯派了只乌鸦向我递话。
t_en: There was no more thrilling escapade than the time Thanatos sent me a raven with a message.

t_ch: 「巴特鲁斯，巴特鲁斯，」它说，「您能偷盗世界上一切东西，唯独偷不到一件。吉奥里亚的秘果，深埋在大地之中，您能如何品尝到它呢？」
t_en: "Bartholos, O Bartholos," it said, "you can steal everything beneath the sky, except for one thing: Georios' secret fruit, buried within the earth. How would you even be able to taste it?"

t_ch: 我于是变成一只钢爪兽，在地里倒腾了无数个日夜，终于吃掉了那颗果实。说实话，味道一般。
t_en: So I turned into a Steelclaw Beast and dug into the earth for countless days, my journey ending with the fruit in my belly. Honestly, it didn't taste like much.

t_ch: 塞纳托斯于是又说：「巴特鲁斯，巴特鲁斯，您能偷盗世界上一切东西，唯独偷不到一件。瑟希斯的秘果，高悬在树庭之上，您能如何品尝到它呢？」
t_en: So Thanatos said again: "Bartholos, O Bartholos, you can steal everything beneath the sky, except for one thing: Cerces' secret fruit, perched atop the Grove. How would you even be able to taste it?"

t_ch: 我于是变成一只飞鸟，一连歇息了十枝枝桠，终于飞到树顶。说实话，味道很一般。
t_en: So I turned into a bird and flew, resting my feet on ten branches, my journey ending at the treetop. Honestly, it didn't taste like much.

t_ch: 塞纳托斯听了哈哈大笑：「巴特鲁斯，巴特鲁斯，你上当了！我吩咐了岩隙的水流、翻墨的乌云，放下了这两颗暗藏『死之核』的果实。」
t_en: Hearing this, Thanatos laughed aloud: "Bartholos, O Bartholos, you have been fooled! I have summoned the currents from craggy crevices, and the clouds of roiling dark to drop these two fruits bearing the 'Core of Death'."

t_ch: 「现在，死亡的果核将在你身体里发芽。你的恶作剧会让大地倾覆、你的玩笑能让生灵涂炭。而这一切，都将成为我的助力。」
t_en: "Now, the seeds of death grow within you. Your pranks will upend the lands, and your tricks will wither all life. And all this will be in my service."

t_ch: 太可怕了，这家伙！
t_en: That's so scary!

t_ch: 我只好回答它：「好险，可是谁吃果子还会吃果核啊？」
t_en: I had no choice but to reply to it: "That is harrowing, but who would eat the seeds along with the fruit?"

t_ch: 听听你最引以为豪的。
t_en: Your proudest feat.

t_ch: 桀桀桀，财物只是最庸俗的目标。可偷的东西还很多…记忆、智慧、乃至身份。
t_en: Crrkrrkk... material wealth is a most trite goal. There are many things to steal... Memories, wisdom, even status.

t_ch: 要窃取它们不用钥匙，也无需撬棍，有时只用几句甜言蜜语，一番动人辞令，就能让人深信不疑。
t_en: Neither keys nor crowbars are needed to steal them. Sometimes all it takes is a glib word, a heartfelt gesture, to have them swoon for your earnestness.

t_ch: 我就是用这个法子，偷到了一个难以想象的秘密…也正因如此，咱现在才得以站在你的面前。
t_en: I used just this method to steal an unimaginable secret... Which is also the reason why I'm standing here before you.

t_ch: 至于您打算把它当作真相，还是一个无伤大雅的吹牛故事，就是您自己的事了。
t_en: Whether you take it as truth or just a harmless tall tale, that's up to you.

t_ch: 关于你的过去…
t_en: About your past...

t_ch: 天哪，您不会是那种人吧？热衷于打听别人的过去，挑出些支离破碎的片段，然后自顾自地断言对方一生都在治愈创伤？
t_en: Gosh, you wouldn't be one of those types, would you? Obsessively digging into one's past, picking out its shattered fragments, and then presumptuously asserting that the other party has been healing their trauma for their whole life?

t_ch: 您要是想装出一副同情我的模样，再假惺惺地劝我从良，那可大可不必。
t_en: If you're thinking of pretending to empathize with me, then pretentiously persuading me to turn over a new leaf, you can save it.

t_ch: 我在过去见过你…
t_en: I have seen you in the past...

t_ch: 和现在很不一样。
t_en: It's very different from your present self.

t_ch: 桀桀桀，您在说什么笑话呢？我，唯唯诺诺、正直礼貌、好奇又充满干劲，被欺负之后，无能狂怒地放着狠话？
t_en: Crrkk, whose leg are you pulling? Me? Obsequious and earnestly polite? Curious and full of energy? Only unleashing harsh words in a fit of powerless rage after being bullied?

t_ch: 您看我和这几个词，有一点搭边吗？
t_en: Do you see any resemblance between me and these phrases?

t_ch: 一、一定是有人冒充吧？或者，也可能是当时我计策的一环…一定是这样的！
t_en: S—Someone must be impersonating me! Or perhaps, it could be one of the steps in my plan back then... That must be it!

t_ch: 你好像有点急了…
t_en: You sound agitated...

t_ch: 闲聊而已，别太慌张。
t_en: We're just chatting, don't get antsy.

t_ch: 我急了吗？您可别胡说。要是有其他想聊的，我乐意奉陪；但要是再对这事喋喋不休，可别怪我要和你比划比划！
t_en: Am I? Nonsense. I am more than happy to acquiesce if you wish to speak on other things. But if you keep persisting on this matter, don't blame me for what happens next!

t_ch: 扎格列斯还说过什么？
t_en: What else did Zagreus say?

t_ch: 扎格列斯老祖留下的名言警句可多着呢，可惜现在的人有眼无珠，只认可瑟希斯的智慧——殊不知，瑟希斯的那点小聪明，都是扎格列斯老祖偷剩下的呢。
t_en: Ancestor Zagreus left many pearls of wisdom. Alas, people nowadays are selectively blind, only heeding Cerces' words — they can't even fathom that the paltry tricks of Cerces are all that remains after Zagreus stole everything else.

t_ch: 您想听什么方面的？
t_en: What do you wish to hear?

t_ch: 来点治愈金句？
t_en: Some adages for the soul?

t_ch: 「智慧让你远离愚钝，愚钝让你靠近幸福。」
t_en: "Wisdom brings you farther from foolishness, but foolishness brings you closer to happiness."

t_ch: 扎格列斯老祖在瑟希斯神体陨灭时如此评价。
t_en: That was what ancestor Zagreus said when Cerces' divine body fell.

t_ch: 来点生活体悟？
t_en: Some quotes about life?

t_ch: 「泰坦不应该有小脚趾，人也不例外。」
t_en: "Titans should not have little toes, and neither should humans."

t_ch: 扎格列斯老祖在逃跑中磕到小脚趾时有云。
t_en: Ancestor Zagreus mentioned this after stubbing its toe while fleeing.

t_ch: 来点日常妙招？
t_en: Got any sayings for life hacks?

t_ch: 「行窃只是取回本该属于自己的东西。」
t_en: "Theft is but retrieving what belonged to you in the first place."

t_ch: 扎格列斯老祖的第一课。
t_en: That's ancestor Zagreus' first lesson.

t_ch: 来点警世恒言？
t_en: Any worldly warnings?

t_ch: 「不要相信奥赫玛人，即便他只是住在奥赫玛城郊。」
t_en: "Do not trust Okhemans, even if they only live in its outskirts."

t_ch: 扎格列斯老祖在被奥赫玛元老们设计陷害时如此评价道。
t_en: Ancestor Zagreus espoused these words when ensnared in a trap set by the Okheman Elders.

t_ch: 不是很感兴趣。
t_en: Not very interested.

t_ch: 尊重您的选择，但您可真是亏大了！
t_en: I shall respect your decision, but you're losing out!

t_ch: 还有别的事。
t_en: Nah, I've got better stuff to do.

t_ch: 那我就不送了——无所事事的午后，也让我闲逛一会儿吧。
t_en: I shan't see you off then. I should be on my walk on this idle afternoon.

t_ch: 晴空、青草、遛弯的公民们…扎格列斯老祖有云：「无所事事的午后，连思考都是罪过。」
t_en: Clear skies, green grass, citizens out and about... Our ancestor Zagreus once said: "The slightest thought is a sin on an idle afternoon."

t_ch: 正事暂且放到一遍，您要是有什么想聊的，咱陪你唠唠也无妨。
t_en: Let's leave that matter aside for now and tell me what you wanted to talk about. I'll banter with you.

t_ch: 关于你的事迹…
t_en: Tell me your story...

t_ch: 哈哈，我的盗窃生涯，比墨涅塔的金丝连起来还长；我偷过的东西，比艾格勒的眼睛更多。
t_en: Haha, my tales of thievery are even longer than Mnestia's golden thread... I've stolen more things than Aquila has eyes.

t_ch: 您想听哪一类，是最有惊无险的、还是我最引以为豪的？
t_en: What do you wish to hear? The most harrowing heist, or my proudest feat?

t_ch: 最有惊无险的吧。
t_en: The most harrowing heist.

t_ch: 最惊险的，莫过于那次——塞纳托斯派了只乌鸦向我递话。
t_en: There was no more thrilling escapade than the time Thanatos sent me a raven with a message.

t_ch: 「巴特鲁斯，巴特鲁斯，」它说，「您能偷盗世界上一切东西，唯独偷不到一件。吉奥里亚的秘果，深埋在大地之中，您能如何品尝到它呢？」
t_en: "Bartholos, O Bartholos," it said, "you can steal everything beneath the sky, except for one thing: Georios' secret fruit, buried within the earth. How would you even be able to taste it?"

t_ch: 我于是变成一只钢爪兽，在地里倒腾了无数个日夜，终于吃掉了那颗果实。说实话，味道一般。
t_en: So I turned into a Steelclaw Beast and dug into the earth for countless days, my journey ending with the fruit in my belly. Honestly, it didn't taste like much.

t_ch: 塞纳托斯于是又说：「巴特鲁斯，巴特鲁斯，您能偷盗世界上一切东西，唯独偷不到一件。瑟希斯的秘果，高悬在树庭之上，您能如何品尝到它呢？」
t_en: So Thanatos said again: "Bartholos, O Bartholos, you can steal everything beneath the sky, except for one thing: Cerces' secret fruit, perched atop the Grove. How would you even be able to taste it?"

t_ch: 我于是变成一只飞鸟，一连歇息了十枝枝桠，终于飞到树顶。说实话，味道很一般。
t_en: So I turned into a bird and flew, resting my feet on ten branches, my journey ending at the treetop. Honestly, it didn't taste like much.

t_ch: 塞纳托斯听了哈哈大笑：「巴特鲁斯，巴特鲁斯，你上当了！我吩咐了岩隙的水流、翻墨的乌云，放下了这两颗暗藏『死之核』的果实。」
t_en: Hearing this, Thanatos laughed aloud: "Bartholos, O Bartholos, you have been fooled! I have summoned the currents from craggy crevices, and the clouds of roiling dark to drop these two fruits bearing the 'Core of Death'."

t_ch: 「现在，死亡的果核将在你身体里发芽。你的恶作剧会让大地倾覆、你的玩笑能让生灵涂炭。而这一切，都将成为我的助力。」
t_en: "Now, the seeds of death grow within you. Your pranks will upend the lands, and your tricks will wither all life. And all this will be in my service."

t_ch: 太可怕了，这家伙！我鼓起勇气回应——
t_en: That was so frightening! I mustered my courage and replied...

t_ch: 「笑死，谁吃果子会吃核儿啊？」
t_en: "Hah, who would eat the seeds of a fruit?"

t_ch: 听听你最引以为豪的。
t_en: Your proudest feat.

t_ch: 桀桀桀，财物只是最庸俗的目标。可偷的东西还很多…记忆、智慧、乃至身份。
t_en: Crrkrrkk... material wealth is a most trite goal. There are many things to steal... Memories, wisdom, even status.

t_ch: 要窃取它们不用钥匙，也无需撬棍，有时只用几句甜言蜜语，一番动人辞令，就能让人深信不疑。
t_en: Neither keys nor crowbars are needed to steal them. Sometimes all it takes is a glib word, a heartfelt gesture, to have them swoon for your earnestness.

t_ch: 我就是用这个法子，偷到了一个难以想象的秘密…也正因如此，咱现在才得以站在你的面前。
t_en: I used just this method to steal an unimaginable secret... Which is also the reason why I'm standing here before you.

t_ch: 至于您打算把它当作真相，还是一个无伤大雅的吹牛故事，就是您自己的事了。
t_en: Whether you take it as truth or just a harmless tall tale, that's up to you.

t_ch: 关于你的过去…
t_en: About your past...

t_ch: 天哪，您不会是那种人吧？热衷于打听别人的过去，挑出些支离破碎的片段，然后自顾自地断言对方一生都在治愈创伤？
t_en: Gosh, you wouldn't be one of those types, would you? Obsessively digging into one's past, picking out its shattered fragments, and then presumptuously asserting that the other party has been healing their trauma for their whole life?

t_ch: 您要是想装出一副同情我的模样，再假惺惺地劝我从良，那可大可不必。
t_en: If you're thinking of pretending to empathize with me, then pretentiously persuading me to turn over a new leaf, you can save it.

t_ch: 我在过去见过你…
t_en: I have seen you in the past...

t_ch: 和现在很不一样。
t_en: It's very different from your present self.

t_ch: 桀桀桀，您在说什么笑话呢？我，唯唯诺诺、正直礼貌、好奇又充满干劲，被欺负之后，无能狂怒地放着狠话？
t_en: Crrkk, whose leg are you pulling? Me? Obsequious and earnestly polite? Curious and full of energy? Only unleashing harsh words in a fit of powerless rage after being bullied?

t_ch: 一、一定是有人冒充吧？或者，也可能是当时我计策的一环…一定是这样的！
t_en: S—Someone must be impersonating me! Or perhaps, it could be one of the steps in my plan back then... That must be it!

t_ch: 你好像有点急了…
t_en: You sound agitated...

t_ch: 闲聊而已，别太慌张。
t_en: We're just chatting, don't get antsy.

t_ch: 我急了吗？您可别胡说。要是有其他想聊的，我乐意奉陪；但要是再对这事喋喋不休，可别怪我要和你比划比划！
t_en: Am I? Nonsense. I am more than happy to acquiesce if you wish to speak on other things. But if you keep persisting on this matter, don't blame me for what happens next!

t_ch: 扎格列斯还说过什么？
t_en: What else did Zagreus say?

t_ch: 扎格列斯老祖留下的名言警句可多着呢，可惜现在的人有眼无珠，只认可瑟希斯的智慧——殊不知，瑟希斯的那点小聪明，都是扎格列斯老祖偷剩下的呢。
t_en: Ancestor Zagreus left many pearls of wisdom. Alas, people nowadays are selectively blind, only heeding Cerces' words — they can't even fathom that the paltry tricks of Cerces are all that remains after Zagreus stole everything else.

t_ch: 您想听什么方面的？
t_en: What do you wish to hear?

t_ch: 来点治愈金句？
t_en: Some adages for the soul?

t_ch: 「智慧让你远离愚钝，愚钝让你靠近幸福。」
t_en: "Wisdom brings you farther from foolishness, but foolishness brings you closer to happiness."

t_ch: ——扎格列斯老祖在成功骗过瑟希斯时如此评价。
t_en: ...Ancestor Zagreus said this after successfully deceiving Cerces.

t_ch: 来点生活体悟？
t_en: Some quotes about life?

t_ch: 「泰坦不应该有小脚趾，人也不例外。」
t_en: "Titans should not have little toes, and neither should humans."

t_ch: ——扎格列斯老祖在逃跑中磕到小脚趾时有云。
t_en: ...Ancestor Zagreus mentioned this after stubbing its toe while fleeing.

t_ch: 来点日常妙招？
t_en: Got any sayings for life hacks?

t_ch: 「行窃只是取回本该属于自己的东西。」
t_en: "Theft is but retrieving what belonged to you in the first place."

t_ch: ——扎格列斯老祖的第一课。
t_en: ...That was ancestor Zagreus' first lesson.

t_ch: 来点警世恒言？
t_en: Any worldly warnings?

t_ch: 「不要相信奥赫玛人，即便他只是住在奥赫玛城郊。」
t_en: "Do not trust Okhemans, even if they only live in its outskirts."

t_ch: ——扎格列斯老祖在被奥赫玛元老们设计陷害时如此评价道。
t_en: ...Ancestor Zagreus espoused these words when ensnared in a trap set by the Okheman Elders.

t_ch: 不是很感兴趣。
t_en: Not very interested.

t_ch: 尊重您的选择，但您可真是亏大了！
t_en: I shall respect your decision, but you're losing out!

t_ch: 还有别的事。
t_en: Nah, I've got better stuff to do.

t_ch: 那我就不送了——无所事事的午后，也让我闲逛一会儿吧。
t_en: I shan't see you off then. I should be on my walk on this idle afternoon.

t_ch: 扎格列斯老爷在上！
t_en: By the Great Zagreus!

t_ch: 欧洛尼斯，别帮倒忙！
t_en: Stay out of this, Oronyx!

t_ch: 翻飞之力！
t_en: The power of whimsy!

t_ch: 尝尝诡计的厉害！
t_en: Taste the power of my schemes!

t_ch: 还你就是…
t_en: I'll give it back to you...

t_ch: 喜欢吗？是我的了！
t_en: You like it? It's mine now!

t_ch: 岁月的大手来了…
t_en: The hand of time is upon us...

t_ch: 放开！放开！
t_en: Let go! Let go!

t_ch: 小偷也是能打的！
t_en: Thieves can fight too, you know!

t_ch: 盗窃失败…
t_en: Theft failed...

t_ch: 贼灵永不失手！
t_en: A master thief never fails!

t_ch: 放弃吧，你追不上的！
t_en: Give up, you'll never catch me!

t_ch: 桀桀桀！这币子赔给你！
t_en: Hehehe! Take these coins as compensation!

t_ch: 桀桀桀，我收下咯！
t_en: Hehehe, it's all mine!

t_ch: 呜哇！糟了糟了！
t_en: Waaa! This is bad!

t_ch: 别小看我！
t_en: Don't underestimate me!

t_ch: 那就从偷改成抢！
t_en: This just changed from a theft to a robbery!

t_ch: 我投降了…
t_en: I surrender...

t_ch: ……是我。
t_en: It's me.

t_ch: 「哟，是您啊。」
t_en: Ooh, it's you.

t_ch: （随便挥手打发它走。）
t_en: (Wave them away.)

t_ch: 先住手——今天我得说说你。我巴特鲁斯的行窃生涯里，还是头回见你这样的人。
t_en: Stop for a moment... I have to tell you something about you. In all of my years of thievery, I have never met someone like you.

t_ch: 这开场白还真是一模一样。
t_en: The same exact introduction, huh?

t_ch: 嗯嗯？怎么回事，我们以前见过面吗？
t_en: Hmm? What do you mean? Have we met somewhere before?

t_ch: 难道我的记性也像欧洛尼斯一样开始不听使唤了？
t_en: Or am I losing my memory like Oronyx?

t_ch: 哦——我知道了。你一定是看了什么赞美我巴特鲁斯伟大职业生涯的戏剧！嗐，这也正常，崇拜我的人确实很多。言归正传……
t_en: Ah — I see! You must have seen a show about my magnificent career! Well, that's natural — I do have many admirers. But anyway...

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 这里面既不是什么价值连城的珠宝，也不是那种稀世罕见的古物。看起来既沉又重，其实不过和这里的众多藏书一样——有没有它们，世界都没什么区别。
t_en: No precious gems or ancient relics in here. Looks heavy and important, but really? Just like these dusty books — the world wouldn't skip a beat without them.

t_ch: 即便如此，你还是要把它们打包扛走吗？是你的钱包拮据到一分钱都不能放过，还是你的脑子空虚到需要廉价的符号填满？
t_en: But you're still gonna haul it away? What's your deal — wallet so thin you're scraping for pennies, or mind so empty you need worthless junk to fill it?

t_ch: 看样子，这箱子很有价值。
t_en: Seems like this chest is actually QUITE valuable.

t_ch: …嗯？被看穿了？
t_en: ...Hm? Caught on, have you?

t_ch: 没、没错！这箱子对于我的价值远超于你！即使这样，你也不肯放弃么？
t_en: Y—you bet! This box means more to me than you could imagine! Still going to fight me on this?

t_ch: 献出片刻生命，本身就有价值。
t_en: Every sacrifice, even the briefest moment of life, carries meaning.

t_ch: 啧…你说话怎么跟这里的祭司似的。还是说，你是那种什么——行为艺术家？
t_en: Tch... you sound just like the priests here. Or are you one of those — what do they call it — performance artists?

t_ch: （笑而不语）
t_en: (Smile silently)

t_ch: 嘶…你这笑容怎么有点瘆人啊。
t_en: Yeesh... that smile of yours is rather disturbing.

t_ch: 因为我就喜欢看你这种反应。
t_en: Cos your reactions amuse me.

t_ch: 哦！那我懂了，你原来是想和我巴特鲁斯做游戏！
t_en: Oh! I get it now — this is all a game to you!

t_ch: 不错不错，这敢情好……
t_en: Now this is getting interesting...

t_ch: 大人的事小孩少管。
t_en: This is grown-up stuff, kid. Run along.

t_ch: 啊？这是什么没大没小的发言？我巴特鲁斯从一滩烂泥里诞生的时候，你爷爷的爷爷还不知道有没有出生在这世上呢！
t_en: Hey, watch that attitude! The universe was young when I came into being a mud puddle — long before your great-great-grandparents were even a twinkle in the Cosmos!

t_ch: 不过这倒也好…这样的你，也许反而是个合格的玩伴？
t_en: Although... perhaps that spark of yours makes you worth keeping around.

t_ch: （笑而不语）
t_en: (Smile silently)

t_ch: 怎么，看不起我？
t_en: Hmph, think you're better than me?

t_ch: 切，那我可是得让你吃惊一下了……
t_en: Then I'll have to surprise you...

t_ch: 好，我巴特鲁斯决定了！这箱子我就让给你——不过别开心太早，只要你拿了这份受诅咒的礼物，世上最烦人的江洋大盗就从此要盯上你啦！
t_en: Alright, I, Bartholos, have decided! You can have this — but don't celebrate too soon. Take this cursed gift, and you'll have the world's most annoying thief master on your tail from now on!

t_ch: 桀桀，以后可有你好看的…就像这满屋子的书，终究也难免付之一炬哦。
t_en: Hehehe, you'll see... Like all these books here, everything turns to ash eventually.

t_ch: 呵呵…不愧是你。连这么拐弯抹角的地方也无法逃脱你的魔爪。
t_en: Hehehe... Not bad at all. Even this secret place couldn't hide from you.

t_ch: 「算啦，这次就也把宝箱展示给你吧。」
t_en: "Perhaps I should show you the chest again."

t_ch: ——桀桀，你以为我会这么说吗？告诉你，这回巴特鲁斯可是为你准备下了天罗地网！做好觉悟吧！
t_en: Hehehe, thought I'd be that nice? This time, Bartholos has set up something special for you! Prepare yourself!

t_ch: 好吧，好吧…没想到聪明反被聪明误，到头来全是自取其辱！
t_en: Well, well... Oh my... Outsmarted at my own game... how humiliating!

t_ch: 这位英雄——敢问您尊姓大名？您若是放我一条生路，我一定逢人便说，您才配坐咱这一行的头把交椅…不对，您就是祖师爷！
t_en: Oh mighty hero — could I get your name? Spare me, and I'll tell everyone you're the best in the business... no wait, you're the legend itself!

t_ch: 不错，识时务者为俊杰。
t_en: Smart move. Know when to yield.

t_ch: 那当然、那当然！我巴特鲁斯以后一定唯您棒首是瞻！您想要什么宝贝，吩咐我去便是，根本不劳您亲自动手！
t_en: Most certainly! I, Bartholos, shall be your devoted servant! Simply name your desired treasures — let my hands do the work!

t_ch: 哦？你这糟老东西坏得很呐……
t_en: Oh? You crafty old devil...

t_ch: 欸，您这说的什么话！我巴特鲁斯当然是真心被您折服，只愿从今以后服侍您棒前球后！
t_en: Come now, such suspicion! I, Bartholos, am genuinely in awe — eager to serve at your command!

t_ch: 只不过…老巴特鲁斯最近手头着实有点紧，眼前的这个宝贝，您能不能高抬贵手，就当是提前发一点工资……
t_en: Although... poor old Bartholos here is rather strapped for coin. Might you consider this treasure an advance on my future services?

t_ch: 啊…好可怜哦…（无动于衷）
t_en: Ah... poor thing... *Shows no sympathy*

t_ch: 想薅我羊毛？没门！
t_en: Think that'll work on me? Think again!

t_ch: 怎么回事？这种骗术对像你这样的大英雄应该很有效才对啊……
t_en: What's going on? This trick usually works like a charm on heroes like you...

t_ch: 啊…我悟了，原来你也和我一样的卑鄙！桀桀桀桀桀……好好好，简直太棒了。这宝箱我可以送给你！
t_en: Aha... Now I see! You're cut from the same cloth as me! Hehehe... Brilliant, absolutely brilliant. Take the chest — consider it a gift between rogues!

t_ch: 有缘再见了，愿扎格列斯老祖平等地祝福我们！
t_en: Until our paths cross again! May the great Zagreus smile upon us both!

t_ch: 哦哟，没有在这{RUBY_B#树庭}树林子{RUBY_E#}里迷路啊，不错不错。
t_en: Oh my, finding your way through {RUBY_B#the Grove}the woods{RUBY_E#} without getting lost? Impressive, impressive.

t_ch: 想想我们也算是老交情了…欸，说起来，{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的力量，你之前用得那么娴熟…该不会在漫长的记忆中，我们早就打过无数次交道了？
t_en: We're old friends by now... And the way you handle {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s power so naturally... Maybe we've danced this dance a thousand times before in their long memories?

t_ch: 别自恋了。
t_en: Don't flatter yourself.

t_ch: 桀桀桀，自恋可是一种美德，巴特鲁斯感谢您的赞赏。
t_en: Hehehe, vanity is a virtue, and I, Bartholos, thank you for the compliment.

t_ch: 你猜。
t_en: Take a guess.

t_ch: 切，没劲。不过你既然这样说，那多半还是有过的吧。
t_en: Hmph, you're no fun. But since you put it that way, I bet we have.

t_ch: 咋搁这儿永恒轮回起来了？
t_en: Why are you doing this eternal cycle conspiracy theory?

t_ch: 看见这黑潮侵袭过的大地，你不会想到沙漠中的秘宝吗？
t_en: Seeing this land ravaged by the black tide, doesn't it remind you of the secret treasure in the desert?

t_ch: 没关系，巴特鲁斯喜欢一切无法确定的关系——箱子里未知的财宝、谁都不知道会发什么芽的种子、还有传说中又死又活的猫。
t_en: But that's fine — I, Bartholos, love all uncertain things: The unknown treasures in chests, seeds that might sprout into anything, and that legendary cat that could be both dead and alive.

t_ch: 「无法确定」包含着一种最深的智慧。我记得扎格列斯老祖就说过……
t_en: Uncertainty holds the greatest wisdom. As great Zagreus used to say...

t_ch: 「智慧是做成你原本不能做成之事的艺术。而我讨厌缺乏智慧的游戏。」
t_en: "Wisdom is the art of achieving what was impossible to you, and I hate games that are without wisdom."

t_ch: 少废话，我的东西你别惦记。
t_en: Cut the chatter. My stuff is off-limits.

t_ch: 诶，别生气嘛，这里没有埋伏，而我也没有你想的那么小气。巴特鲁斯只是想起了一则那位老祖的故事……
t_en: Easy there, no need to be upset. There are no traps here, and I'm far more generous than I seem. I simply recalled a story about that great ancestor...

t_ch: 你和扎格列斯到底是什么关系？
t_en: What exactly is your relationship with Zagreus?

t_ch: 巴特鲁斯是贼灵，{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}翻飞之币{RUBY_E#}是巴特鲁斯的祖师爷和老朋友，这不是显然的吗？
t_en: As a Spirithief, I, Bartholos, am blessed to call the {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Coin of Whimsy{RUBY_E#} both ancestor and friend. Rather obvious, no?

t_ch: 据说，为了扼制扎格列斯的恶作剧，其他泰坦在它身上绑了一颗星星，每当扎格列斯从天上逃走，贼星的轨迹就会暴露它的方向。
t_en: Legend has it that the other Titans tied a star to Zagreus to stop their pranks. Every time Zagreus escaped through the sky, the Thief Star would mark their trail.

t_ch: 但其实，扎格列斯把星星系到了一块石头上，当它想要恶作剧的时候，就会把石头扔出去！桀桀桀，让泰坦们为了找贼星而错过<color=#dbc291ff>眼皮子底下</color>的诡计……
t_en: But here's the trick — Zagreus attached the star to a stone. So whenever they wanted to do mischief, they'd simply toss the rock! Hehehe! Sending the Titans on a wild chase after the Thief Star while missing what's <color=#dbc291ff>right under their noses</color>...

t_ch: 好啦，这种子本身也不是我想偷的东西，就大方地送给你吧。这树林子里只能进、不能出的道路不止一处，你可千万别阴沟翻船搞迷了路啊？
t_en: Tell you what. I wasn't really after this seed anyway, so consider it yours. Just remember — the Grove's paths often lead you in, but not back out. Mind you, don't lose your way!

t_ch: 识得这里是什么吗？一位泰坦的心脏！桀桀桀，若是能做得「偷心大盗」，我巴特鲁斯也算是不枉此生呀……
t_en: Recognize this place? You're standing in a Titan's heart! Hehehe, if I could manage to steal that, I, Bartholos, would go down in history as the greatest thief of all...

t_ch: 你也就嘴上爽爽。
t_en: You're all talk.

t_ch: 嘴上爽爽怎么了…{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}它老人家大人大量，一定不会在意的。
t_en: So what? The great {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} surely wouldn't mind.

t_ch: 怎么，终于不惦记我的宝箱了？
t_en: Oh? Not interested in my treasure chest anymore?

t_ch: 一码归一码！再说，这明明是无主的宝箱，你我都是用智慧和气力在争夺——怎么就是你的了？
t_en: That's another thing! Besides, it's unowned. Both of us claimed it by wit and strength — why is it yours?

t_ch: 心与心同字，令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: Melding of hearts, eh? Funny, isn't it?

t_ch: 那是那是，我巴特鲁斯还是很幽默的。
t_en: Exactly. I, Bartholos, have quite the sense of humor.

t_ch: 哦…对了对了，说到{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}——你知道吗，它最早其实是{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的一缕头发……
t_en: Speaking of {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}, did you know they originally came from a strand of {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s hair...?

t_ch: 从前，巴特鲁斯听说这个故事的时候，也想剪一缕自己的头发试试，看看能种出什么。你猜怎么着？
t_en: I once thought of trying the same — cutting my hair to see what would grow. But guess what?

t_ch: ——巴特鲁斯突然意识到自己根本没有头发！桀桀桀桀。
t_en: I suddenly realized I had no hair! Hahaha!

t_ch: 没错没错，就像我说的，诡计在泰坦的<color=#dbc291ff>眼皮子底下</color>！
t_en: Indeed, as I said, the tricks are under the Titan's <color=#dbc291ff>nose</color>!

t_ch: 不过嘛，桀桀桀桀……
t_en: However, hehehehe...

t_ch: ——诡计当然得包含偷袭！
t_en: Tricks must involve surprise attacks!

t_ch: 不错不错，身手矫健，技艺非凡——这个从泰坦眼皮子底下偷走的宝箱属于你啦！
t_en: Fine skills and agility — the treasure chest stolen from under the Titan's nose is yours.

t_ch: 这么直接就承认失败？
t_en: Admitting defeat so easily?

t_ch: 当然，这不过是游戏的一部分。诡计如果蔓延到游戏之外，那肯定不是什么好事。
t_en: Of course, it's all part of the game. Tricks that go outside the game would be a problem.

t_ch: 我银河球棒侠，天下无敌！
t_en: I am the Galactic Baseballer, and I'm invincible!

t_ch: 那是，那是…你早就是这个游戏的大师级玩家了。
t_en: That's right... You've already become a master of this game.

t_ch: 老巴特鲁斯一直都有这样的人生哲学——诡计如果蔓延到游戏之外，那肯定不是什么好事。
t_en: I, Bartholos, have a philosophy: Tricks taken outside the game would be a problem.

t_ch: 又来，我的东西你少惦记。
t_en: Not again. Stop eyeing my stuff.

t_ch: 切，小气。真正的江洋大盗都讲究一个见者有份，像你这样一毛不拔的我巴特鲁斯就没见过第二个！
t_en: Stingy! Real thieves share spoils when they work together. I've never seen anyone as tight-fisted as you!

t_ch: 不过，这的确也是游戏的一部分，就这么着吧！
t_en: Fine. It's all just a game, anyway.

t_ch: 回想起来，扎格列斯老祖当年也说过：「一切的最终意义都在游戏当中。只有游戏能够让它的意义指向自己本身。」
t_en: Even ancestor Zagreus once said: "The ultimate meaning lies within the game. Only the game can make its meaning point back to itself."

t_ch: 扎格列斯这么有文化？
t_en: Zagreus? That's profound of them.

t_ch: 嗯？诡计之泰坦也是泰坦吧？如果说的都是大白话，那还怎么维持泰坦的威严啊？
t_en: Of course! The Trickery Titan is a Titan, right? If they're all just talking nonsense, then how do Titans maintain their dignity?

t_ch: 听起来不像扎格列斯的口吻。
t_en: That tone doesn't sound like Zagreus.

t_ch: 唔，也许这确实不是扎格列斯老祖说的，而是巴特鲁斯自己说的？
t_en: Hmm, maybe it wasn't what Zagreus said, but what I said?

t_ch: 不对不对，我巴特鲁斯才不会这么说话。所以一定是扎格列斯老祖说的！
t_en: No... I, Bartholos, would never speak like that. It must have been what Zagreus said!

t_ch: 好啦，我们也是老交情了…拿着你的战利品，认真投入你自己的游戏吧！我们还会再见面的…桀桀桀桀。
t_en: Alright, we're old friends now... Take your treasure and devote yourself to your own game! We'll meet again... Hehehehe...

t_ch: ——和我抢宝箱也就算了，现在连张写满废话的旧纸，您也揪着不放了？
t_en: ...Trying to snatch chests loaded with treasure from me is one thing, but an old scrap of paper covered in nonsensical scribbles? You won't even let something like that go?

t_ch: 怎么，难道您对「艾格勒的启目集」也感兴趣？
t_en: What, are you also interested in "Aquila's Enlightenment" or something?

t_ch: 我可是考据党。
t_en: I'm a lore collector.

t_ch: 失敬失敬，不过对着这些玩意儿，能考据出些什么呢？
t_en: My apologies, I didn't realize. But what is there even to collect with junk like this?

t_ch: 看到能交互就点了…
t_en: When I see an option to interact, I just press it...

t_ch: 桀桀，没错，就是这种心情！老巴特鲁斯我看到宝箱，也控制不住自己的手呢！
t_en: Hehe, now that's what I'm talking about! When I see a treasure chest, I can't control my own hands either!

t_ch: 看你吃瘪就很高兴！
t_en: Nothing pleases me more than seeing you humiliated.

t_ch: 桀桀，我欣赏你的坏心眼！您有一颗多么纯粹的心灵呀……
t_en: Hehe, I can admire that kind of wickedness! Truly, what a pure heart you have...

t_ch: 唉，要不是云石市集的一个誊写商找上门来，我才懒得碰这些老掉牙的废纸呢。
t_en: *sigh*... I'd never even bother with antiquated scrap paper like this if it weren't for that scribe from Marmoreal Market who came knocking.

t_ch: 那帮{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}的信徒看到启目集被偷，就像要了老命似的，忙不迭筹钱找人补上。
t_en: Whenever those followers of {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#} see the Enlightenment stolen, they act like it's the end of the world or something, scurrying around to raise money to complete the set again.

t_ch: 老巴特鲁斯我偷走一本，那誊写商就多一笔生意——咱俩两两分账，实在是财源滚滚呀！只可惜，好好的生意又被你搅黄咯。
t_en: Every book old Bartholos steals, the scribe gets another commission — and we split the profit! We were just rolling in cash! A never-ending river of money! Though, you just had to dam it up.

t_ch: …还能入伙吗？
t_en: ...Is it too late for me to join your little venture?

t_ch: 想入这行？你还嫩着呢！
t_en: Ha! You wanna join our line of business? You're still too wet behind the ears, {F#girl}{M#boy}.

t_ch: 这也太过分了！
t_en: That's beyond the pale!

t_ch: 老巴特鲁斯填饱了肚子，誊写商养活了一家老小，信徒的虔诚得到了证明——简直是三全其美的好事呀！
t_en: Old Bartholos gets to fill his stomach, the scribe can feed his family, and the believers get a chance to prove their piousness... It's a win-win-win!

t_ch: 您就拿着这卷纸考究去吧，我去掏掏那帮朝圣者的兜好了。像{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}老祖说的——
t_en: You take this scroll here and go investigate, and I'll go rifle through those pilgrims' pockets. Just like venerable old {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#} said:

t_ch: 「不要放过朝圣者，他们的兜里总能掏出东西！」
t_en: "Never let pilgrims off the hook, they've always got something in their pockets!"

t_ch: 偶尔我会想象自己是一只蝴蝶…能飞多远并不是它最关心的事，它只在乎自己能不能自由自在地舞蹈。
t_en: Sometimes I imagine myself as a butterfly... It's not about how far you can fly — it's about dancing freely through the air.

t_ch: 当然，飞不过沧海的蝴蝶多少降低了游戏的难度——所以你应该能猜到我接下来要做什么了？
t_en: Of course, having a butterfly that can't cross the vastness makes this too easy — so you can probably guess what I'm about to do!

t_ch: 谁知道呢？
t_en: Who knows?

t_ch: 嗐，看在我们老交情的份上，快开始吧！
t_en: Come on, for old times' sake, let's get started!

t_ch: 奉陪到底！
t_en: I'm with you till the end!

t_ch: 桀桀，等的就是你这句话！
t_en: Hehe, that's exactly what I wanted to hear!

t_ch: 累了，赶紧把宝箱还我……
t_en: I'm beat. Just hand over the chest already...

t_ch: 俗话说，盗亦有道…没点仪式感可怎么行！
t_en: As they say, even thieves have their principles... We've got to do this with style!

t_ch: 哎呀哎呀，看着你在战斗中的曼妙身姿，也是如蝴蝶起舞一般呐！
t_en: My my, your graceful fighting style — it's like watching a butterfly dance!

t_ch: 过奖过奖……
t_en: You flatter me...

t_ch: 诶，谦虚可不是江洋大盗的美德。
t_en: Hey, modesty doesn't suit a master thief.

t_ch: 要骄傲、要自满、要自我意识爆棚——只有这样才能欺骗一切、偷走一切、掠夺一切！桀桀桀桀……
t_en: Be proud, be confident, let your ego run wild — that's how you deceive all, steal all, and plunder everything! Hehehe...

t_ch: 直接把宝箱给我不就得了。
t_en: Just give me the chest and be done with it.

t_ch: 诶，这就是你的不对了。我要是每次都老老实实把东西给你，那这游戏还有什么意思？
t_en: Now, now. If I just quietly handed things over every time, where's the fun in that?

t_ch: 人们都说讲故事追求一个「意料之外，情理之中」，其实做游戏也是如此呀。
t_en: They say good stories should be "unexpected yet reasonable" — making games is just the same.

t_ch: 你喝的什么假酒？
t_en: What kind of bootleg liquor have you been drinking?

t_ch: 酒不醉人人自醉！老巴特鲁斯没什么别的爱好，就好这口…桀桀桀。
t_en: It's not the wine that intoxicates — it's people themselves! Old Bartholos here has no other pastimes, just a fondness for this... Hehehe.

t_ch: 看见那边闷不做声的{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}了吗？
t_en: Notice that silent {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} over there?

t_ch: 那老伙计，一言不发多没意思…这世界上除了一致向阳的植物，总归还要有点醉醺醺、不守规矩的动物不是？
t_en: That old fellow, so silent and dull... This world needs more than just plants growing toward the sun — we need some tipsy, unruly creatures too, don't you think?

t_ch: 所以——还是得靠咱们来当那万绿丛中一点红，你说是吧。桀桀。
t_en: So... we've got to be that pop of red in the sea of green, wouldn't you say? Hehehe.

t_ch: 桀桀，没想到吧，这里也有我巴特鲁斯的身影。
t_en: Hehehe, didn't expect to run into me here, did you?

t_ch: 人们都说，{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}老祖能在死人的世界来去自由——但要我说，这事情也没他们讲得那么特殊。
t_en: They say ancestor {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#} could freely traverse the world of the dead — but honestly, it's not as remarkable as they make it out to be.

t_ch: 比方说…伙计，你有没有想过，一个「死人的世界」究竟意味着什么？
t_en: Think about it, have you ever pondered what a "world of the dead" actually means?

t_ch: 绝妙的藏宝地。
t_en: An excellent place to stash treasures.

t_ch: 那是自然，毕竟钱财对于死者毫无意义！不过说到这个，巴特鲁斯也有自己的困惑……
t_en: Of course, since riches mean nothing to the dead! But that reminds me, I've been pondering something myself...

t_ch: 死人究竟有什么东西能值得一偷？桀桀，要是知道这个，就可以把它藏到「活人的世界」去！
t_en: What do the dead own that's worth stealing? Hehehe, if we figured that out, we could hide it in the "world of the living"!

t_ch: 灵魂不灭。
t_en: The soul is eternal.

t_ch: 桀桀，所以偶尔死一死也没什么关系！但是啊，你说有没有这样一种可能……
t_en: Hehehe, so dying occasionally isn't the end of the world! But listen, what if...

t_ch: 咱们就算轮回转世了，结果要过的日子也和上辈子大差不差？有时候还真是越想越觉得恐怖呐。
t_en: What if we reincarnate but end up living pretty much the same life as before? The more I think about it, the more frightening it gets.

t_ch: 让我能活着遇见你。
t_en: So I could meet you while still alive.

t_ch: 啊？突然这么肉麻做什么，巴特鲁斯难道和你有什么特殊的伙伴关系吗？
t_en: Huh? Why get all mushy now. Do we have some kind of special connection?

t_ch: 哦…等等。等等等等。所以，你的意思是，你「从一开始就已经死了」？桀桀桀桀，那还真是出乎意料之外啊！
t_en: Oh... wait. Wait just a minute. You mean you've "been dead from the very beginning"? Hehehe, now that's something I didn't see coming!

t_ch: 总之，既然我们在这里见面了，巴特鲁斯就送你一句金玉良言……
t_en: Well, since our paths have crossed here, allow me to offer some pearls of wisdom...

t_ch: 咱们这个世界啊，活着的人不像活着，死了的人不像死了——但只有骗子永远都是骗子，因为他就算说了实话，你们也没人敢信！桀桀桀桀。
t_en: In our world, the living don't quite seem alive, and the dead don't quite seem dead — but a trickster is always a trickster, because even when telling the truth, nobody dares trust them! Hehehe.

t_ch: 桀桀，来这里看看老巴特鲁斯的精彩杰作。
t_en: Hehehe, behold! A great work of old Bartholos.

t_ch: 它从大海深处的无底洞里偷出了一颗巨大的原石；又为了鉴定其中是不是真的蕴含着一块充满魔力的水晶，所以把这块石头升到了天上，好劈开看看究竟……
t_en: This huge raw stone was pilfered from the bottomless depths of the sea. Then, to determine if it really held a crystal filled with magical power, the stone was lifted to the sky to split it open and peer inside...

t_ch: 得了吧，你又不是泰坦……
t_en: Stop it. You're no Titan...

t_ch: 诶，你算得泰坦，我就算不得泰坦？老伙计，人还是要发挥一下想象力嘛——我在现实中不是泰坦，那也不妨碍我在故事里是个泰坦嘛。
t_en: Hey, if you can call yourself a Titan, why can't I? Old buddy, let's use some imagination here — I may not be a Titan in real life, but that doesn't mean I can't be one in stories.

t_ch: 所以天上这东西值多少钱？
t_en: So how much would that thing up there fetch?

t_ch: 价值连城！当然，前提是你能想办法把它搬走…到时候就算是随便找个山之民把它丢进炉子里给化了，那也能炼出不少金子吧！
t_en: It's invaluable! Assuming you can find a way to move it... Even if you just had some Mountain Dweller melt it down, you'd still get a good amount of gold out of it!

t_ch: 别闹了，快把宝箱给我。
t_en: Stop playing games and give me the chest.

t_ch: 啧啧，你这老伙计可真无趣。
t_en: Tsk, you're no fun at all, old buddy.

t_ch: 好啦，刚才咱也只是随口瞎编个故事…这里毕竟是跟凡尘俗世完全不同的冥界，鬼才知道天上挂着的究竟是些什么——不过风景是真漂亮。
t_en: Fine, I was just spinning a tale... This is the nether realm after all, worlds apart from the mortal realm — who knows what those things in the sky actually are? Though I must admit, the view is absolutely killer.

t_ch: …嗯？等等。我是不是不经意间讲了一个冷笑话？既然是冥界，那熟悉这里情况的家伙当然也只能是「鬼」了嘛，桀桀桀桀……
t_en: ...Hmm? Wait, did I just stumble into a deadly joke? Well, I suppose there wouldn't be any living being left to tell the tale with this "killer" on the loose, hehehe...

t_ch: 巴特鲁斯这会儿很虚无。你知道为什么吗？
t_en: You know what? My mind is going places it shouldn't. Care to take a guess?

t_ch: 不知道。
t_en: No idea.

t_ch: 桀桀，不愧是我的老伙计。因为巴特鲁斯自己也想不清楚。
t_en: Hehehe, good one, old buddy. That's exactly what's going on in my mind. Nada.

t_ch: 咱只是有种古怪的感觉，总觉得世界上其实有许许多多的{NICKNAME}和巴特鲁斯，每一组他们都玩着同样的寻宝游戏。
t_en: I just have this weird feeling that there are countless {NICKNAME}s and Bartholoses in existence, each pair playing the exact same treasure-hunting game.

t_ch: 这究竟是好事还是坏事？或者说，巴特鲁斯是不是脑子坏了才突然产生了这样莫名其妙的想法？
t_en: What do you think? Is this a blessing or a curse? Or maybe I've just gone crazy to suddenly think such odd thoughts?

t_ch: 喂，老伙计——我们打一架吧，互相确认一下彼此是不是真实的存在！
t_en: Hey, old buddy — let's fight to verify our existence!

t_ch: 因为你是巴特鲁斯。
t_en: Because you're Bartholos.

t_ch: …太棒了，你说得对啊！
t_en: ...Brilliant, you're absolutely right!

t_ch: 巴特鲁斯只是一只什么都做不到的贼灵…想做的事情有那么那么多，能办到的却那么那么少。
t_en: I'm just a helpless Spirithief... with endless ambitions, but so little power to achieve them.

t_ch: ——事已至此，也只有打一架能让我找回点活着的实感啦，桀桀！
t_en: In that case, only a real fight can make me feel alive again, hehehe!

t_ch: 快交出宝箱！
t_en: Hand over the chest now!

t_ch: 啧。你这个老伙计，怎么一点都不可怜老巴特鲁斯的精神状态呐！可恶，来打一架！
t_en: Tsk. You're a cold one, not caring at all about your old pal Bartholos's state of mind! Alright then, let's fight!

t_ch: 呼…在内心虚无的时候，能被暴揍一顿还真有点舒服。
t_en: Phew... there's something oddly satisfying about getting beaten up when feeling empty inside.

t_ch: 这里也曾是一座美丽的城邦吧？可只要世界的齿轮稍加转动，灰飞烟灭，也不过是弹指瞬间……
t_en: This used to be a magnificent city-state, right? But with just a turn of the cosmic wheel, it all vanished in a moment...

t_ch: 太可恶了，到处都是冥界的脏水，搞得巴特鲁斯在这里根本没法找到真正的宝藏！
t_en: This is maddening — the nether realm's foul water is everywhere, making it impossible for me to find any real treasure here!

t_ch: 把大家叫出来就为了这个啊。
t_en: So that's what this was all about.

t_ch: 喂喂，我们都玩了这么多次了，就偶尔让你听听老巴特鲁斯的抱怨，应该也不是什么罪过吧？
t_en: Come on now, after all our games together — surely it's not too much to ask you to listen to old Bartholos's complaints occasionally?

t_ch: 所以，你的身份其实是……
t_en: So, your true identity is...

t_ch: 嗯？你啥意思，巴特鲁斯不就是巴特鲁斯吗？
t_en: What do you mean, my true identity? I'm Bartholos!

t_ch: 倒是你，能继承{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的神力…要说只是普通的异邦人或者活死灵，这谁信啊？
t_en: But for someone who can inherit {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s power... who'd believe you're just a regular traveler or living dead?

t_ch: 等等等等，还有隐藏的宝藏？
t_en: Wait a minute. There's hidden treasure?

t_ch: 唔…那可不一定吧？如果连老巴特鲁斯都不知道那东西在哪，也许它就真的从来没有存在过……
t_en: Well, that's... debatable. If even old Bartholos doesn't know where it is, maybe it never existed to begin with...

t_ch: 或者，是平行时空里的巴特鲁斯这么做了，然后我不小心下载了它的信息？…桀桀，那巴特鲁斯不就成传信石版了？还真是意想不到的展开呢！
t_en: Or maybe a Bartholos from another spacetime did it, and I accidentally downloaded their information? ...Hehehe, that would make "Bartholos" a teleslate! What an unexpected twist!

t_ch: 咳咳…想当年，{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}老祖有句话是怎么说的来着……
t_en: Ahem... What was it that ancestor {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#} used to say...?

t_ch: 「世界是命运的重复叠加。所谓的真相，往往远没有诡计来的公平。」
t_en: "The world is a stack of recurring destinies. Truth is often less fair than trickery."

t_ch: …嗯？老祖它说话会这么沉稳吗？也可能没有吧，时间太久，巴特鲁斯有很多东西都记不真切了。
t_en: ...Hmm? Would they really talk so cohesively like that? Maybe not. It's been so long. I can't remember things clearly anymore.

t_ch: 不过你不一样！{NICKNAME}——巴特鲁斯可不会忘了这个有趣的名字，桀桀桀桀……
t_en: But you're different, {NICKNAME}! I won't forget such a fascinating name, hehehe...

t_ch: 好啦好啦，还给你还给你……
t_en: Okay, okay, you can have it back...

t_ch: 假一赔十，豪华版雅努斯密径，轻松使用不必对焦！
t_en: Guaranteed authentic or your money back tenfold! Deluxe Janus's Hidden Passage — user-friendly, no focusing required!

t_ch: 没想到你还挺有格调？
t_en: Huh, didn't expect you to be such a connoisseur.

t_ch: 那是。好歹我们一起玩了这么久，就算没有爱情、友情，那也有亲情了不是？
t_en: Of course! We've spent so much time together that even without love or friendship, we've developed family-like bonds, haven't we?

t_ch: 帮我把其他密径也都解了。
t_en: Solve all the other Hidden Passages for me too.

t_ch: 嗐，那你也太高看巴特鲁斯了，咱又不是那{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}本尊……
t_en: Hah, that's giving old Bartholos too much credit. I'm not the {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} in person...

t_ch: 呃，我丢的不是这个……
t_en: Uh, that wasn't what I lost...

t_ch: 你丢了一个铁斧头，我还你一个金斧头，这不是很正常嘛。
t_en: You dropped an iron axe, I'm returning a golden one. That's how it works, isn't it?

t_ch: 说起来，泰坦们总是这样…从来不想着好好说话，只会设计各种谜题来刁难我们。按理说，根据「再创世」的观点，它们的前身应该也是人类吧？怎么就这么没有人性呢……
t_en: Titans are always like this, you know? Never straightforward, always making riddles to torment us. According to "Era Nova" theory, they were human once too, right? So why are they so utterly inhumane...

t_ch: 你提到了再创世？
t_en: Did you just say "Era Nova"?

t_ch: 啊？有吗有吗？巴特鲁斯不懂那种高深的学问，你应该是听错了吧！
t_en: What? Did I? Bartholos doesn't comprehend such advanced concepts — you must have heard wrong!

t_ch: 啧，还说你不是扎格列斯……
t_en: Tsk, yet you insist you're not Zagreus...

t_ch: 嗯？扎格列斯老祖也不是我这种口风不严的主吧？唔…不对不对，有时候我的口风确实还挺严的！桀桀桀桀……
t_en: Hmm? Would ancestor Zagreus be this indiscreet? Wait... that's not right. Sometimes I can be extremely discreet! Hehehehe...

t_ch: 行了行了，这里也没我什么事了。拜拜了您！祝你冒险愉快。
t_en: Well, that's all from me, then. Farewell! Enjoy your adventure.

t_ch: 哎呀哎呀，刚才真是好大的风，吹得咱脚都站不稳了。来来来，快来扶咱一把！
t_en: Goodness gracious, what a gust that was! Nearly knocked me over. Come on, give me a hand!

t_ch: 现在站稳不就好了。
t_en: You seem stable enough already.

t_ch: 啧。冷酷无情的{F#女人}{M#男人}！咱们也好久没打过架了，想必手痒痒了吧？开练开练！
t_en: Tsk. You cold-blooded {F#woman}{M#man}! We haven't dueled in forever — bet you're just dying for some action! Let's rumble!

t_ch: 总觉得你想偷袭我……
t_en: I suspect you're setting up an ambush...

t_ch: 嚯。不愧是您，一下儿就看穿了巴特鲁斯的诡计！那恭敬不如从命，我们就速速开始试炼吧！
t_en: Ha! Just like you to instantly see through my trick! Well then, let's get this trial started immediately!

t_ch: 你有脚？
t_en: Wait, you have feet?

t_ch: 孤陋寡闻！没听说过吗，后手也是手，前脚也是脚…咳咳，为了挽回名誉权，巴特鲁斯只好向你提出决斗！
t_en: So uncultured! Haven't you heard, any hind-hand is still a hand, and any front-foot still a foot... Ahem, to restore my honor, Bartholos must challenge you to a duel!

t_ch: 桀桀桀，你的身手果然越来越好啦！能有你这样的侠义之士保护着翁法罗斯，我巴特鲁斯就算是香消玉殒…咳咳，咱这是在胡说些什么啊。
t_en: Hehehe, your skills have gotten impressive! With a valiant hero like you protecting Amphoreus, I, Bartholos, can rest in peace... Ahem, what nonsense am I spouting?

t_ch: 不过，架都打了，您不妨就听老巴特鲁斯再讲个小故事吧。
t_en: Now that we've dueled, how about letting Old Bartholos here spin you a quick yarn?

t_ch: 从前，有个不知哪来的外地富商在奥赫玛大摆筵席，一连摆了一百多天——都是免费自助！随便什么人，只要搞到了他的请柬，就可以进去大吃大喝，看演员唱歌跳舞，玩一整天都行。
t_en: Once, a wealthy foreign merchant from parts unknown hosted an extravagant feast in Okhema for over a hundred days — completely free! Anyone with an invitation could enter, feast lavishly, watch entertainment, and enjoy themselves all day.

t_ch: 人们都说，这位富商真是富可敌国，历史上那些最伟大的商人，恐怕也未必能与他相比。
t_en: They said this merchant was so incredibly rich that he could rival entire nations — perhaps even the greatest historical merchants paled in comparison.

t_ch: 但一百多天之后，或许是看不惯他这样公开收买民心，又或是纸醉金迷的生活产生了什么纠纷，元老院里有人刺杀了他。富商既死，人们自然好奇他留下了多少遗产——
t_en: Yet after a hundred days, perhaps out of disdain for his public way of buying people's hearts, or due to conflicts arising from the decadent lifestyle, someone from the Council of Elders assassinated him. With the merchant dead, people naturally wondered how much wealth he had left behind—

t_ch: 可所有人找来找去，楞是没找到富商剩下的一分利衡币。
t_en: Yet despite thorough searches, not even one Balance Coin of his wealth could be located.

t_ch: 现在请你猜猜，这位富商的财富究竟去了哪里？
t_en: Now I ask you: Where did the merchant's riches disappear to?

t_ch: 多半是在你肚子里。
t_en: Probably sitting in your stomach right now.

t_ch: 吼吼吼…原来你是这么看呀。
t_en: Ohh... So that's your theory, is it?

t_ch: 从一开始就是你的诡计。
t_en: You planned this whole thing from the start.

t_ch: 桀桀桀…原来你是这么看呀。
t_en: Hehe... So that's your theory, is it?

t_ch: 干嘛在编的故事里找答案？
t_en: Why seek truth in a story you just invented?

t_ch: 呀呀呀…原来你是这么看啊。
t_en: Ahh... Is that how you see it?

t_ch: 这个问题嘛，确实没有标准的答案——扎格列斯老祖讲给巴特鲁斯的时候就没有。不过，巴特鲁斯倒是觉得…它未必不是某种历史事件的预演？
t_en: Indeed, this question has no definitive answer — ancestor Zagreus offered none when telling Bartholos. Still, Bartholos thinks... it might be a preview of actual historical events?

t_ch: 咳咳咳…好像一不小心暗示得有点多了。天机不可泄露！拜拜！
t_en: Ahem... I might've let slip too many hints. Some secrets must remain hidden! Bye now!

t_ch: 咳咳，是你啊。巴特鲁斯说过，你是它的好朋友…原来如此。
t_en: Ahem, it's you. Bartholos has spoken of you as its good friend... Now I understand.

t_ch: …你不是巴特鲁斯？
t_en: ...Wait, you're not Bartholos?

t_ch: 我是巴特鲁斯吗？我不是巴特鲁斯吗？我也会和你玩诡计游戏，所以我是巴特鲁斯。但巴特鲁斯现在很忙，实在没有时间玩游戏，所以我不是巴特鲁斯。
t_en: Am I Bartholos? Or am I not? I play tricks like Bartholos, so I am Bartholos. But Bartholos is too busy for games right now, so I am not Bartholos.

t_ch: …我是不是被刚才那个虫子精神污染了？你知道的，人如果太嘴硬，非要说自己什么都知道，就容易闹出笑话。总之我不是巴特鲁斯，我是那家伙的分身。
t_en: Did that bug's nonsense affect my mind? You know how it goes — insist you know everything and you'll end up looking foolish. Anyway, I'm not Bartholos — I'm its projection.

t_ch: 它还有分身的能力？
t_en: So it can create projections of itself?

t_ch: 如你所见，我无论外貌、名字、声音、还是性格，都和它大差不差，那它的分身能力想必是不错的吧！桀桀桀桀。
t_en: Just look at me — nearly identical in appearance, name, voice, and character. Its projection abilities must be impressive! Hehehehe.

t_ch: 它到底忙什么去了？
t_en: What exactly is it busy doing?

t_ch: 这我可不知道，我还想问你呢。那家伙平常总愿意亲力亲为，像这样分出我来替它做游戏还真的少见。
t_en: I wish I knew — I was going to ask you the same thing. It normally likes doing things itself, so making me to play games in its stead is pretty rare.

t_ch: 它恢复了泰坦的力量？
t_en: Did they recover their Titan powers?

t_ch: 泰坦？什么泰坦…桀桀桀，你不会被它给骗了吧！「巴特鲁斯其实是扎格列斯？」桀桀桀，也只有扎格列斯老祖自己才会一本正经的这么胡说八道了！
t_en: Titan? What Titan... Hehehe, don't tell me you believed that! "Bartholos is actually Zagreus?" Hehehe, only ancestor Zagreus would say such ridiculous things with a straight face!

t_ch: 咳咳，现在反正游戏也结束了，虫子我就还给你吧！不过我其实还挺好奇，泡了一次黄金池，它还愿不愿意说自己什么都知道…桀桀。
t_en: Ahem, the game's over, so here's your bug back! I do wonder if after its pool of gold experience, it'll still brag about knowing everything... Hehe.

t_ch: 有人知道自己知道，有人知道自己不知道，有人不知道自己知道，有人不知道自己不知道。不过还有一种人，他们知道自己不知道自己不知道——那才是最厉害的。
t_en: Some know they know, some know they don't, some don't know they know, and some don't know they don't. But the most impressive are those who know they don't know they don't know.

t_ch: 我的话已经是最简洁的了，真没法再缩减了喀。
t_en: I've already made it as simple as possible — can't possibly trim it down more, you know.

t_ch: 咳咳，是你啊。巴特鲁斯说过，你是它的好朋友…所以这里会有一场试炼。
t_en: Ahem, it's you. Bartholos has spoken of you as its good friend... which means there will be a trial here.

t_ch: 你也是分身？
t_en: Another projection, huh?

t_ch: …也？哦，想必你刚刚见过其他的巴特鲁斯分身。没关系，只要不考虑人格的同一性，我们就都是巴特鲁斯。
t_en: Another? Oh, you've met my fellow projections. Well, if we ignore the whole identity issue, we're all Bartholos anyway.

t_ch: 话虽如此，巴特鲁斯仍然给我设定了命运，也就是在这里和你进行试炼。准备好，要开始了！桀桀桀桀……
t_en: Still, Bartholos assigned me a purpose — to test you in this trial. Ready yourself! We begin now! Hehehehe...

t_ch: 试炼没意思，我不要打。
t_en: Trials are no fun. I'm not fighting.

t_ch: 我也不想打…但巴特鲁斯给我设定的命运就是要和你进行试炼。没办法，快点来吧，早点结束早点下班！
t_en: I don't want to fight either... but Bartholos assigned me to test you. Can't be helped — let's get this over with quickly so I can call it a day!

t_ch: 最喜欢揍你了，快点来罢！
t_en: There's nothing I love more than pummeling you — bring it on!

t_ch: 我也最喜欢被你揍——才怪！
t_en: And I just love getting pummeled — YEAH RIGHT!

t_ch: 啊啊，巴特鲁斯说得没错，你果然很强。也许已经超过当年制霸此地的阳雷骑士了吧！
t_en: Ah, Bartholos wasn't exaggerating — you're incredibly strong! You might even outclass the Daythunder Knight who once reigned supreme here!

t_ch: 不过阳雷骑士赢得过{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}，却未必能赢过{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}…这么想来还是你比较强！溜了溜了，我要去和自己的真身会合了！
t_en: The Daythunder Knight beat {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#} but probably couldn't defeat {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#}... which makes you the stronger one! Time for me to return to my original form!

t_ch: ——哦对，{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的谜题我已经帮你解好了，直接用就行，不客气！
t_en: Oh right! I solved {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}'s Passage for you. It's ready to be used. No thanks necessary!

t_ch: 桀桀，巴特鲁斯说得没错，这里果然出现了数据异常。啊…灰色头发的，你就是巴特鲁斯的那位忘年之交，咱说的没错吧？
t_en: Hehe, Bartholos was right — we found a data anomaly here. Ah... gray-haired one, you must be Bartholos' friend from across the ages, yes?

t_ch: 你也是分身？
t_en: Another projection, huh?

t_ch: 当然。就像刚才傻转着的那只，巴特鲁斯的分身和分身亦有不同。「人生是一座医院」，扎格列斯老祖说过，「每个病人都被调换床位的事情缠住。」
t_en: Obviously. Like that silly spinning one earlier, Bartholos creates different kinds of projections. "Life is a hospital," ancestor Zagreus said, "where every patient is fixated on switching beds."

t_ch: 真巴特鲁斯在哪？
t_en: Where's the real Bartholos?

t_ch: 黎明机器都完蛋了，你说它现在在哪？当骗子的诡计失灵，它也就只能献上自己的小命！桀桀桀桀……
t_en: Now that the Dawn Device is bust, where else would it be? When a trickster's plans collapse, only its life remains to give! Hehehehe...

t_ch: 不许吃我的陀螺！
t_en: Stop eating my spinning top!

t_ch: 陀螺？桀桀桀桀，它那个原地打转的样子的确很像陀螺！拥有这样的幽默感，不愧是巴特鲁斯传说中的朋友。
t_en: Top? Hehehehe, it really did look like a spinning top, didn't it! With that sense of humor, no wonder you're Bartholos' legendary friend.

t_ch: 言归正传，我之所以要把刚才那只吞掉，不过是回收一些数据罢了。也不知应该高兴还是悲伤…越是接近再创世，那种野生的愚蠢数据就越是泛滥。要是你再看到它们……
t_en: To be frank, I only absorbed that one to recover data. I'm torn between joy and sorrow... as Era Nova approaches, these wild, stupid data fragments multiply. If you see more of them...

t_ch: 无论你喜不喜欢，大概也只能陪它们一起玩了吧！桀桀桀……祝你好运，咱临走给你刷个大宝贝！
t_en: Like it or not, you're stuck playing along with them! Hehehe... Good luck! I'll leave you a big prize before I go!

t_ch: 桀桀，巴特鲁斯大人之挚友！吾非巴特鲁斯大人，但吾明白，汝在彼之心里有特殊地位。
t_en: Hehe, noble friend of Lord Bartholos! I am not Lord Bartholos, yet I know of thy special standing in his affections.

t_ch: 看守宝箱之怪物吾已清理，汝可安享收益。巴特鲁斯大人曾告诫吾等…有一天外之神秘生物，名唤「策划」，专司吾等命运。
t_en: The monsters guarding the chest have been dealt with. Enjoy your spoils. Lord Bartholos once warned us... A mysterious being from beyond, known as the "Developers," governs our fate.

t_ch: 可那「策划」不似泰坦，不行保境安民、乐善好施之举，反倒一味安排吾等刁难客人、将善缘恶戏……
t_en: But unlike Titans, these "Developers" don't safeguard realms or perform good deeds — they merely orchestrate our harassment of guests and corrupt good intentions into cruel jokes...

t_ch: 你人设没问题？
t_en: Is your character setting okay?

t_ch: 吾是巴特鲁斯大人的书袋，是它在历史长河中吞下的无用知识，是面目可憎的一块胃结石。换言之——
t_en: I am Lord Bartholos' book collection, the pointless knowledge it accumulated through ages, a grotesque gallstone in its belly. That is to say—

t_ch: 吾的人设很有问题，乃BUG是也。
t_en: My character setting is deeply flawed — a genuine BUG.

t_ch: 说人话。
t_en: Speak plainly.

t_ch: 吾乃巴特鲁斯大人之分身，在此恭候多时，陪您享受游戏。
t_en: I am Lord Bartholos' projection, dutifully waiting here to entertain thee with this game.

t_ch: 真巴特鲁斯在哪？
t_en: Where's the real Bartholos?

t_ch: 巴特鲁斯大人雄才伟略，此去突然，想必只为「再创世」耳。
t_en: The talented and strategic Lord Bartholos — its abrupt departure must certainly concern "Era Nova."

t_ch: 缘分似潮，有风乃起。命路已断，此功玉成。桀桀。
t_en: Destiny flows like tides, rising with the winds. The path of fate is broken, this work now complete. Hehe.

t_ch: 这里原本存放着「艾格勒的启目集」，然而如今只剩下意味不明的污言秽语。
t_en: "Aquila's Enlightenment" was once stored here, but now only vulgar nonsense remains.

t_ch: 您好，我是巴特鲁斯的一个分身，我现在有些悲伤。
t_en: Hello there, I'm a Bartholos projection, and I'm feeling quite melancholic.

t_ch: 咋，你还信艾格勒？
t_en: You still believe in Aquila?

t_ch: 哦不不不，您误会了。我悲伤的，是世界即将拉下帷幕，而我们就像「启目集」一样被主人抛弃。
t_en: Oh no, you've got it wrong. I'm sad because the world is ending, and we'll be discarded by our master — just like "Aquila's Enlightenment" was.

t_ch: 我们的母体巴特鲁斯…它自己去完成英雄之旅，却把我们弃置于此，等待着一个或许永远无法等到的访客。
t_en: Bartholos's main body... left to complete its Hero's Journey, abandoning us to wait for a visitor who may never arrive.

t_ch: 万幸的是，您来了。这本应令人高兴，可一旦想到老巴特鲁斯为什么要这么做，我就再次悲伤起来……
t_en: Thankfully, you came. This should make me happy, but when I ponder why old Bartholos did this, my sorrow returns...

t_ch: 巴特鲁斯曾经也偷过这个。
t_en: Bartholos has pilfered this before.

t_ch: 是吗？很可惜，我并不具备那样的记忆…因为我只是一个由母体巴特鲁斯捏出的空壳，寿命恐怕也仅此一天。
t_en: Really? Too bad I don't have those memories... I'm just an empty shell created by Bartholos, probably lasting only this one day.

t_ch: 我诞生的唯一目的就是在这里等待您的到来…啊，多么沉重的承诺，多么自作多情的遗产……
t_en: I was created for one purpose: to wait here for you... ah, what a heavy obligation, what a self-important inheritance...

t_ch: 真巴特鲁斯在哪？
t_en: Where's the real Bartholos?

t_ch: 它不在任何地方。可它也无所不在。它出发地很匆忙，你也许能从其他伙伴那里打听到它最后的故事。
t_en: It's nowhere and everywhere at once. It left in a rush — you might hear its final story from other companions.

t_ch: 老巴特鲁斯的朋友很少。但您是它极重要的朋友。愿{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}翻飞之币{RUBY_E#}祝福你们在再创世之后相见。桀桀。
t_en: Old Bartholos has few friends, but you are most precious to it. May the {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Coin of Whimsy{RUBY_E#} ensure you meet again after Era Nova. Hehe.

t_ch: 桀桀，想我了吗？很可惜，我不是巴特鲁斯本人，只是它投下的一道幻影。
t_en: Hehe, did you miss me? Unfortunately, I'm not Bartholos in person, merely a shadow it projected.

t_ch: 不过巴特鲁斯和我讲过那个搬运工的故事，它说和你在这里玩角色扮演真的非常开心。
t_en: But Bartholos told me about the porter story and said it had a blast role-playing with you.

t_ch: 只是，翁法罗斯是个很小的世界，即使诡计多端的巴特鲁斯也渐渐发现自己没有新的角色可以扮演。但你——巴特鲁斯最好的朋友之一，却依然乐意和它游戏。
t_en: But Amphoreus is a tiny world, and even the tricky Bartholos ran out of roles to play. Yet you — one of their best friends — still enjoyed playing along.

t_ch: 这让巴特鲁斯非常感动，也让它在面对某些命运的时候，更多了一份坦然。
t_en: This touched Bartholos profoundly, helping it face certain destinies with more acceptance.

t_ch: 别煽情，我只是为了宝箱。
t_en: Cut the sentimentality, I just want the chest.

t_ch: 做事论迹不论心，承认吧，您和巴特鲁斯早就是要好的朋友了。
t_en: We judge by actions, not thoughts. Face it, you and Bartholos became good friends long ago.

t_ch: 它的确是一位特别的朋友。
t_en: They truly are a special friend.

t_ch: 桀桀，巴特鲁斯如果能听见这句话，它一定会很高兴吧！
t_en: Hehe, that would make Bartholos so happy if they heard you!

t_ch: 真巴特鲁斯在哪？
t_en: Where's the real Bartholos?

t_ch: 唔，被我吃了？桀桀，开玩笑的。
t_en: Hmm, maybe I ate them? Hehe, only joking.

t_ch: 哎呀，此时此刻，我真想代替老巴特鲁斯说一句——「啊，黎明陨落后的月色真美」——可惜这里不像{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}灰黯之手{RUBY_E#}的领域，根本没有什么月亮。
t_en: Ah, I wish I could say for old Bartholos  —  "How lovely the moon looks after dawn's fall"  —  but sadly, unlike the {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Hand of Shadow{RUBY_E#}'s realm, there's no moon here.

t_ch: 所以，还是算了吧！我即将动身去和真正的巴特鲁斯会合，而你…不出意外，你将见证翁法罗斯最后、也是最初的命运。桀桀，掰掰！
t_en: So, never mind! I'm off to rejoin the real Bartholos, and you... if all goes as planned, you'll witness Amphoreus' last — yet also first — destiny. Hehe, see ya!

t_ch: 桀桀桀桀，老巴特鲁斯每次被您逮住，原来就是这个感受啊！
t_en: Hehehehe, now I know exactly how old Bartholos feels when you catch it!

t_ch: 如您所见，咱是它老人家的一个分身，只是因为有点古道热肠，所以在协助奇美拉和小海豹们避难。不过既然被您如此捉住，那咱想必是好心办了坏事——
t_en: As you see, I'm one of its projections, but being somewhat kindhearted, I'm helping the chimera and baby seals escape. But since you've caught me, I must have done wrong while meaning well—

t_ch: 咱知道错了！求求您，看在老巴特鲁斯的薄面上，大人有大量，放过咱吧！
t_en: I admit my mistake! Please, for old Bartholos' sake, show mercy and spare me!

t_ch: 放过你是可以……
t_en: I could spare you...

t_ch: 不过是不是漏了什么？
t_en: But isn't something missing here?

t_ch: 漏？桀桀桀桀，您在说什么呐，咱可没有趁机把这位奇美拉的私房钱……
t_en: Missing? Hehehehe, what are you suggesting? I definitely didn't steal this chimera's secret stash...

t_ch: …啧，可恶，说漏嘴了。没办法，那就休怪咱无情无义——
t_en: Tsk, curses, I blurted it out. Well then, don't blame me for what follows—

t_ch: ——还请您乖乖闭上嘴吧！
t_en: I must insist you remain silent!

t_ch: 啊啊啊…这就是天外来客的实力吗！老巴特鲁斯常说的不打不相识…竟然是单方面挨揍的意思吗……
t_en: Aaaaah... this is the power of visitors from beyond the sky! Old Bartholos always claimed fighting creates bonds... did it mean getting pummeled?

t_ch: 这要是战场，你早死了。
t_en: On a real battlefield, you'd be dead by now.

t_ch: 我大人不记小人过。
t_en: I'm generous enough to forgive your petty crimes.

t_ch: 别闹了，把宝箱给我。
t_en: Stop the games and give me the chest.

t_ch: 是是是…咱早应该知道，对你们这种名为「主角」的生物来说，任何人的私房钱都相当于你们自己的财宝……
t_en: Yes, yes, of course... I should've known that for creatures called "protagonists," everyone's savings are basically your rightful treasures...

t_ch: 扎格列斯老祖在上，这种目标咱没法用诡计去骗啊！呜呜呜，老巴特鲁斯，我要回到你的身体里，再也不出来了……
t_en: Ancestor Zagreus help me, I can't trick someone like you! Waaah, old Bartholos, I want to return to your body and never leave again...

t_ch: ——嗷！嗷呜呜！
t_en: Oww! Awoooo!

t_ch: 虽然很像你的那些小小调皮朋友，但这的确是另一只独立的奇美拉。你们素未谋面，因此也很难理解对方的语言。
t_en: Despite resembling your playful little friends, this is a completely different chimera. Since you've never met, you can't understand each other's language.

t_ch: 嗷呜……
t_en: Awooooo...

t_ch: 看得出，它在比划着什么。你发挥充分的想象力，开始在脑海中理解它的意图……
t_en: You can see it's trying to convey something. With your imagination at full power, you start understanding its meaning...

t_ch: （假设台词）可恶！可恶的贼灵！它骗我们说，只要把财宝放进它的肚子里，就能让亿万家产躲过再创世的洗礼，让我们在下一世赢在起跑线上！
t_en: (Invented dialogue) Damnations! That conniving Spirithief! It convinced us that putting our treasures in its belly would protect our wealth through Era Nova, giving us an advantage in our next life!

t_ch: （假设台词）唉…果然还是要谨记泰坦的教诲，钱财乃身外之物…世界马上就要重启了，我是用不到这些私房钱了，就全送给你吧！说不定你的口袋反而能把它们带进下一世的翁法罗斯！
t_en: (Invented dialogue) Alas... I should've followed the Titans' teaching that riches are temporary... with the world about to restart, these savings are useless to me, so take them all! Maybe your pockets can bring them into the next Amphoreus!

t_ch: …嗷呜。
t_en: ...Awoo.

t_ch: 小奇美拉用真诚的眼神看着你，不再言语。
t_en: The little chimera stares at you with sincere eyes, falling silent.
